summary: Added compatibility scaffolding for m2c valid-syntax output and adjusted unknown function prototypes so headers can serve both normal project compilation and m2c context generation. The PR introduced src/m2c_macros.h with M2C_UNK types and placeholder macros for m2c-emitted constructs, added UNK_T/UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS abstractions in placeholder.h, switched a group of fighter header prototypes from concrete void(void) placeholders to unknown return/parameter macros, and made small platform/header changes for ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT, ARRAY_SIZE, memcpy/m2c support, and __frsqrte declaration placement. The stated goal was to avoid the old unk_t/any_t split and move toward UNK_T plus void*, while still allowing m2c to infer types effectively under M2CTX.
what_changed: Created src/m2c_macros.h for macros emitted by m2c in --valid-syntax mode, including M2C_UNK, M2C_UNK8, M2C_UNK16, M2C_UNK32, M2C_UNK64, M2C_FIELD, M2C_BITWISE, unaligned-read placeholders, unhandled-instruction placeholders, M2C_CARRY, and memcpy pattern aliases.

Updated src/placeholder.h to include m2c_macros.h and define UNK_T, UNK_RET, and UNK_PARAMS differently depending on M2CTX: m2c context generation sees M2C_UNK and empty parameters, while normal compilation sees void* or void/void prototypes.

Changed many unknown prototypes in src/melee/ft/code_80081B38.h from void func(void) to UNK_RET func(UNK_PARAMS), preserving normal build behavior while exposing less misleading unknown signatures to m2c.
Added ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT to src/Runtime/platform.h so __restrict is used only for MWERKS outside M2CTX, and expands away otherwise.
Changed string.h prototypes for strncpy and strcpy to use ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT instead of raw __restrict, making the header friendlier to non-MWERKS and m2c contexts.
Changed math.h to include MetroTRK/intrinsics.h and removed the local extern double __frsqrte(double) declaration, implying the intrinsic declaration should come from the MetroTRK header.
smart_moves: Used preprocessor gates around M2CTX rather than weakening normal project prototypes globally. This lets normal compilation keep void foo(void) semantics while m2c gets empty parameter lists for inference.
Centralized m2c valid-syntax support in a dedicated m2c_macros.h instead of scattering ad hoc definitions across source files.
Defined unknown-size aliases such as M2C_UNK8, M2C_UNK16, M2C_UNK32, and M2C_UNK64 so m2c output can compile while still recording the inferred storage width.
Introduced ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT as a portability shim, avoiding raw __restrict in contexts where clang or m2c context generation may not accept or benefit from it.
Made the unknown prototype convention explicit with UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS, documenting the distinction between real known void functions and functions whose return/arguments are not yet understood.
decomp_lessons: Do not mark unknown functions as void func(void) just because the current decompilation lacks a signature; that can suppress useful m2c inference and misrepresent unknown parameters or return values.

For m2c context generation, unknown types should prefer M2C_UNK, or at least a long-like scalar, because m2c can infer better from unknown scalar placeholders than from void* or overly concrete C signatures.
When a header must satisfy both the real build and m2c context generation, use conditional macros keyed on M2CTX to present different but compatible signatures.

m2c --valid-syntax output may emit macro placeholders for unknown fields, reinterpret casts, unaligned accesses, unhandled instructions, carry bits, and memcpy patterns; keeping these macros available makes generated code compilable without immediate hand-editing.
Restrict qualifiers and compiler-specific keywords should be hidden behind platform macros when headers are consumed by multiple compilers/tools.
Prefer central header abstractions for unknowns, e.g. UNK_T, UNK_RET, and UNK_PARAMS, over maintaining parallel unk_t/any_t conventions.
naming_conventions: Use uppercase UNK_T for project-level unknown data types, with normal compilation mapping to void* and M2CTX mapping to M2C_UNK.
Use UNK_RET for an unknown return type; this maps to void for normal compilation and M2C_UNK for m2c context generation.
Use UNK_PARAMS for unknown parameter lists; this maps to void for normal compilation and an empty parameter list under M2CTX.
Use M2C_UNK, M2C_UNK8, M2C_UNK16, M2C_UNK32, and M2C_UNK64 for m2c-facing unknown scalar widths.
Use M2C_* prefix for macros that directly correspond to m2c valid-syntax emitted constructs.
Use ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT instead of spelling __restrict directly in shared headers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Support m2c type inference by exposing unknown prototypes as UNK_RET func(UNK_PARAMS) under M2CTX instead of locking them to void func(void).

Use m2c --valid-syntax mode together with m2c_macros.h so generated code remains compilable even when it contains unresolved fields, bitwise casts, unaligned loads, unhandled instructions, or carry-bit placeholders.
Keep normal build semantics intact while generating more permissive m2c contexts by making UNK_PARAMS expand to empty only for M2CTX.
Use M2C_FIELD(expr, type_ptr, offset) for m2c-emitted unknown field accesses of the form pointer-plus-offset cast/dereference.

review_feedback.evidence: The PR had one review and no review comments in the provided context.
review_feedback.feedback: No specific reviewer feedback was available in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace unk_t or any_t with UNK_T or void*.
Find later uses of UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS in fighter headers.
Find PRs that modify src/m2c_macros.h after PR 721.
Find m2c context generation fixes involving M2CTX, -requireprotos, or --valid-syntax.
Find headers that still use raw __restrict instead of ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT.
